source: NITI Aayog, National Multidimensional Poverty Index: A Progress Review 2023
method: Headcount ratio (share of population who are multidimensionally poor) by State/UT, NFHS-5 (2019-21), from the report's State/UT headcount-ratio chart. MPI is a composite of deprivation in health, education and living standards (nutrition, cooking fuel, housing, assets, electricity, sanitation, water).
note: Multidimensional poverty captures exactly the deprivations that make heat lethal, kutcha housing, no cooling fuel, poor assets, weak health, so it is a sharper vulnerability lens than an income line. For context, the World Bank's old extreme-poverty line of $1.90 a day (2011 PPP) is roughly 150-160 rupees a day at today's exchange rate, far less in real terms; about a tenth of Indians fell below it around 2019.

byState.state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
